extension.valueMarkdown:
Public health is notified. Contact investigation initiated. Robert is started on RIPE therapy (rifampin, isoniazid, pyrazinamide, ethambutol). After clinical improvement he's discharged to outpatient directly observed therapy (DOT) at the affiliated TB clinic.

Two sites, one health system, one state (IL). This tests Permission Ticket constraints on period (only the investigation window) and scopes (public health may only need specific resource types like Condition, DiagnosticReport, Observation for lab results).

resource.conclusion: 3.5 cm cavitary lesion in the right upper lobe with surrounding parenchymal infiltrate. Mildly enlarged right hilar lymph nodes. No pleural effusion. Radiographic appearance highly suspicious for pulmonary tuberculosis.

Normocytic anemia, mild (anemia of chronic disease)

reasonCode.text: Chronic cough with hemoptysis, weight loss, night sweats

extension.valueMarkdown: Robert James Davis, a 65-year-old Black male, presented to the University of Illinois Hospital Emergency Department via walk-in at approximately 14:20 on January 22, 2026. He reported a two-month history of progressively worsening productive cough, now occasionally blood-streaked, accompanied by drenching night sweats (2–3 nights per week), anorexia, and unintentional weight loss of approximately 15 lbs.

reasonCode.text: Pulmonary tuberculosis, active — diagnosis confirmation and RIPE therapy initiation

extension.valueMarkdown: Robert was admitted directly from the ED to a negative-pressure isolation room on the inpatient medicine service at approximately 18:00 on January 22, 2026. The admission encompassed a 5-day hospital stay through discharge on January 27, 2026.

dosageInstruction.text: 500 mg by mouth as needed for pain (HELD during admission)

reasonCode.text: Held during admission to simplify hepatic monitoring while on hepatotoxic TB medications

note.text: Held during admission to simplify hepatic monitoring while on hepatotoxic RIPE therapy. Do not resume without provider approval.

dosageInstruction.text: Take 3 tablets (1200 mg total) by mouth once daily
Take 300 mg by mouth once daily
4 mg IV as needed for nausea

note.text: Administered twice on hospital day 2 (01/23/2026) with relief

dosageInstruction.text: Take 3 tablets (1500 mg total) by mouth once daily
Take 50 mg by mouth once daily

reasonCode.text: Peripheral neuropathy prophylaxis during isoniazid therapy

dosageInstruction.text: Take 600 mg by mouth once daily
